ENGNew: Dam Supporters' Couple Falls to Their Deaths
changeReason: Improved translation from Japanese. Old: "Dam Supporter Couple's Falling Accident"

TextENGNew: From the evening edition, June XX, 1980.

Around 2 PM on the XXth, an office worker named XXX-san and his wife XXXX-san from Hinamizawa village, X district, Shishibone City, fell 27 meters from an observation deck into a mountain stream within the prefectural Shirakawa Natural Park and went missing.
changeReason: New translation adds 'X district' address detail and specifies 'prefectural' for Shirakawa Natural Park, matching the Japanese source; also restructures the sentence for better clarity on the sequence of events.

TextENGNew: Police and fire department personnel searched downstream and discovered the body of XXX-san around 7 PM that same evening.
His wife, XXXX-san, has yet to be found.
The mountain stream had swollen due to the recent Typhoon No. 3, making the search extremely difficult.
changeReason: Original mistranslates 台風３号 as 'category 3 typhoon' (severity rating); new translation correctly renders it as 'Typhoon No. 3' (the third named typhoon of the season).

TextENGNew: It appears the couple had been leaning against the railing at the observation deck when it gave way, causing them both to fall.
changeReason: New translation adds 'It appears' to reflect the speculative tone (模様) in the Japanese, and changes 'fence' to 'railing' — a more contextually appropriate rendering for an observation deck.

TextENGNew: The railing had deteriorated with age, and police are questioning relevant parties to determine whether the park's facilities had been properly maintained.
changeReason: Original implies park staff weren't taking 'adequate precautions' (personnel focus); new translation correctly focuses on whether facilities were 'properly maintained' (設備管理が適正), matching the Japanese emphasis on equipment management.
